mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 in high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between GDPC1 and UNRATE fails to explain >15% of SPX return variance in expansionary periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 for combined proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) indicates supply shocks, causing stagflationary pressures that reduce S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in stagflation regimes exceed 20d forward return thresholds in >40% of cases, or interaction effect explains <10% of return variance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market demand, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in >35% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 in tight labor regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Slumping housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400k indicate weak demand for construction, reducing economic activity and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns exceed 20d forward thresholds in >50% of cases when HOUST is below 1400, or Rank IC < 0.03 in contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising corporate earnings (RSAFS) signals overcapacity and profit margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between INDPRO and RSAFS explains <12% of SPX return variance in overcapacity phases, or Rank IC < 0.04 for combined proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) during strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates labor market mismatch, creating uncertainty that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns exceed 5d forward thresholds in >45% of cases with UNRATE >4% and PAYEMS >150k, or interaction effect explains <8% of return variance.
